beats.activity: Wakes up, makes coffee, checks weather and ferry schedules out of habit.
beats.reasoning: Aria starts her Monday with full energy. Early wake allows her to ease into the day and enjoy the sunny forecast—perfect for planning an outdoor errand or leisure activity later.

beats.activity: Drives to pottery studio to drop off a finished piece and pick up clay for a new project.
beats.reasoning: Aria values creative pursuits; pottery is a core hobby. Running this errand early avoids crowds and lets her get it done before the workday. Wallingford is a reasonable cross-town drive from Madison Park.

beats.activity: Drives downtown to Capitol Hill steakhouse, navigating around Light Rail closure between Westlake and University District.
beats.reasoning: The Light Rail maintenance closure (9am–3pm) forces a drive rather than transit; Aria's risk-averse nature means she leaves extra buffer to avoid being late. She arrives with time to spare.

beats.activity: Pre-service prep: reviews wine inventory, trains new staff on pairings, sets up the bar for lunch service.; Works lunch service during USA vs Wales FIFA World Cup match; bar is packed with sports fans ordering wine and appetizers.
beats.reasoning: As sommelier, Aria handles pre-service duties before the 11am FIFA rush. The USA vs Wales match brings early bar traffic; her restaurant will be busy.

beats.activity: Grabs a quick lunch and decompresses during the post-match lull; sarcastic texts to a close friend about the chaotic FIFA crowd.
beats.reasoning: Aria takes her break on-site, eating what the kitchen offers. She prefers small friend circles over big crowds, so she vents to a close friend rather than mingling with the FIFA crowd.

beats.activity: Afternoon service: manages wine selections for dinner guests, handles reservations and wine pairings.
beats.reasoning: Afternoon shift continues into dinner prep. Aria stays focused on her core sommelier duties—upselling and advising on wine pairings for the evening crowd.

beats.activity: Commute home by car, taking advantage of the sunny evening and clear roads.
beats.reasoning: Light Rail closure is now over (ended at 3pm), but Aria prefers driving anyway. She leaves right after afternoon service and heads home early to enjoy the sunny Monday evening.

beats.activity: Prepares a light dinner at home, then heads to Green Lake for an evening walk and to scout fly-fishing spots for an upcoming trip.
beats.reasoning: Perfect weather (74F, sunny) draws Aria outdoors for her beloved fly-fishing hobby. Green Lake is a short drive from home and offers both leisure and practical trip planning. She's energized and wants to maximize the daylight.

beats.activity: Returns home, settles in with a glass of wine, works on pottery sketches for her next piece.
beats.reasoning: Aria winds down at home with her two passions: wine (professional knowledge) and pottery design. This is a natural transition into evening relaxation, keeping her in the home zone before sleep.

diary: Monday started quiet—coffee at home, then pottery studio in Wallingford to drop off a piece that finally felt right. The clay dust always grounds me. Drove downtown dodging the Light Rail closure, which felt like the whole city was rerouting itself. The steakhouse exploded at lunch. FIFA World Cup, USA vs Wales, and suddenly everyone wanted wine with their wings. I trained a new staffer on pairings between pouring for screaming sports fans, which was honestly kind of hilarious. Texted my friend some choice sarcasm about it all. By afternoon service things settled, and I could actually focus on wine selection again—my favorite part. The evening drive home was gorgeous, sun still high. Green Lake called to me, so I walked the perimeter scouting fly-fishing spots for a trip I'm planning. There's something about that light on the water in June. Got home, sketched pottery ideas with a glass of wine. Felt full, you know?
thought_process: Today felt like a rhythm I needed. The pottery drop-off grounded me before the chaos—I knew the steakhouse would be slammed with the World Cup match, so that small creative moment mattered. The Light Rail closure barely registered; I just adjusted my route automatically. During lunch service, I could've been frustrated by the noise and crowd, but I leaned into the absurdity instead—that's where the humor saved me. The afternoon allowed me to do actual sommelier work, which recharged me. My mood shifted from grounded-and-ready to frazzled-but-coping to finally-peaceful by evening. The Green Lake walk wasn't random; I needed to move my body and remember there's life beyond the restaurant. Wine sketches afterward felt like the perfect close—two creative outlets in one day, which is rare and needed.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
